title: Zeltnera breviflora
taxonomy: Zeltnera breviflora is a species within the plant family Gentianaceae. This plant is classified as a member of the genus Zeltnera, which is a part of the larger Gentianaceae family. The Gentianaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the gentian or gentian family. Zeltnera breviflora is native to western North America and is often found in mountainous regions. The classification of Zeltnera breviflora is still being studied and refined by botanists.
uses: Zeltnera breviflora, also known as dwarf blue-eyed Mary, is a plant with reported medicinal uses. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. intro: Zeltnera breviflora, also known as Short-flowered Penstemon or Short-flowered Beardtongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Zeltnera breviflora has been associated with various health benefits, including its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant's bioactive constituents, which include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Zeltnera breviflora is commonly used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications, to harness its potential benefits for digestive health and skin issues.
